message:
test(resolv): smoke-fuzz proptests for ResolverConfig::parse (bd-s170)

Three smoke-fuzz proptests for /etc/resolv.conf parsing — the
highest-value fuzz target in the resolver module because a panic on
malformed input crashes every process that touches the resolver.

fuzz_resolv_parse_never_panics
  Arbitrary 0..4096-byte sequences. Must never panic.

fuzz_resolv_parse_clamps_hold_for_arbitrary_bytes
  Regardless of input, the returned config must respect every
  documented limit: nameservers≤MAX_NAMESERVERS, search≤MAX_SEARCH_DOMAINS,
  ndots≤15, timeout∈[1,30], attempts∈[1,5]. A regression that
  forgot to clamp "options ndots:999" would fail here.

fuzz_resolv_parse_directive_salad_never_panics
  Biased strategy: random permutations of 17 known directive shapes
  (valid and out-of-range). Reaches the actual directive handlers
  instead of the keyword-reject fast-path that raw-byte fuzz hits
  almost exclusively.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
